ddls:
CREATE TABLE "country_data" (
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "country_region" TEXT /* Name of the country or region */,
  "confirmed" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases of COVID-19 */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total deaths due to COVID-19 */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total recovered cases from COVID-19 */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Total active cases of COVID-19 */,
  "new_cases" INTEGER /* Number of new cases reported */,
  "new_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of new deaths reported */,
  "new_recovered" INTEGER /* Number of new recoveries reported */,
  "deaths_per_100_cases" REAL /* Deaths per 100 confirmed cases */,
  "recovered_per_100_cases" REAL /* Recovered cases per 100 confirmed cases */,
  "deaths_per_100_recovered" REAL /* Deaths per 100 recovered cases */,
  "confirmed_last_week" INTEGER /* Confirmed cases reported last week */,
  "one_week_change" INTEGER /* Change in confirmed cases over the last week */,
  "one_week_percent_increase" REAL /* Percentage increase in confirmed cases over the last week */,
  "who_region" TEXT /* WHO region classification */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country or region */,
  "hospitalized" INTEGER /* Current number of hospitalized patients */,
  "icu" INTEGER /* Current number of ICU admissions */,
  "vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of vaccinated individuals */,
  "vaccine_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccine used */,
  "testing_rate" REAL /* Testing rate per 100,000 people */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_data_who_region FOREIGN KEY ("who_region") REFERENCES who_regions ("region_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "who_regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each WHO region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the WHO region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Description of the WHO region */,
  "region_population" INTEGER /* Population of the WHO region */,
  "region_area" REAL /* Geographical area of the WHO region in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health metric entry */,
  "country_region" TEXT /* Name of the country or region */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of health metric (e.g., confirmed, deaths, recovered) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the health metric */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the report */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_metrics_country_region FOREIGN KEY ("country_region") REFERENCES country_data ("country_region")
)

CREATE TABLE "weekly_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weekly report */,
  "country_region" TEXT /* Name of the country or region */,
  "confirmed_last_week" INTEGER /* Total confirmed cases reported last week */,
  "new_cases_last_week" INTEGER /* Total new cases reported last week */,
  "new_deaths_last_week" INTEGER /* Total new deaths reported last week */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of the report */,
  "recovered_last_week" INTEGER /* Total recoveries reported last week */,
  "testing_last_week" INTEGER /* Total tests conducted last week */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weekly_reports_country_region FOREIGN KEY ("country_region") REFERENCES country_data ("country_region")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES country_data ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
